summary: P0 Multi-Dashboard System Testing Complete - All features verified and working. Backend APIs (24/24 tests passed) and Frontend UI (all components verified). Dashboard Builder at /settings/dashboards displays 4 system dashboards with correct widget counts. IAM User form includes Dashboard Type dropdown with all dashboard options.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/dashboards/list returns 4 system dashboards
Backend: Admin Executive Dashboard has 19 widgets
Backend: Resolver/Operator Dashboard has 11 widgets
Backend: Partner/Vendor Dashboard has 9 widgets
Backend: Requester/Client Dashboard has 10 widgets
Backend: GET /api/dashboards/widgets returns widget library with categories
Backend: Widget library contains KPI Cards, SLA Status, Pool Status, Charts, Ticket Lists
Backend: POST /api/dashboards/{id}/clone creates cloned dashboard
Backend: GET /api/dashboards/{id}/preview?role=Administrator returns preview
Backend: Preview filters widgets by role permissions
Backend: POST /api/dashboards creates custom dashboard
Backend: PUT /api/dashboards/{id} updates dashboard
Backend: DELETE /api/dashboards/{id} fails for system dashboards (400)
Backend: DELETE /api/dashboards/{id} succeeds for custom dashboards
Backend: User creation with dashboard_type_id works
Backend: User update with dashboard_type_id works
Backend: User response includes dashboard_type_name
Backend: All widgets have required_permissions field
Backend: Pool widgets require orders.pick or orders.view permission
Frontend: Dashboard Builder page loads at /settings/dashboards
Frontend: 4 system dashboards displayed with correct widget counts
Frontend: Dashboard Editor opens with Widget Library (drag-drop)
Frontend: Widget Library shows categories: KPI Cards, SLA Status, Pool Status, My Workload, Charts, Ticket Lists, Other
Frontend: IAM page loads with Add User button
Frontend: Add User dialog has Dashboard Type dropdown
Frontend: Dashboard Type dropdown shows all 4 system dashboards
Frontend: Dashboard Type dropdown shows default role for each dashboard
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard Builder uses @dnd-kit for drag-drop widget reordering
System dashboards cannot be deleted but can be edited
Each widget has required_permissions for auto-hide functionality
Dashboard Type is a required field in IAM user form
Preview API correctly filters widgets based on role permissions
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - 4 system dashboards auto-created by backend
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-Dashboard System is complete. 4 system dashboard templates exist: Admin Executive (19 widgets), Resolver/Operator (11 widgets), Partner/Vendor (9 widgets), Requester/Client (10 widgets). Dashboard Builder at /settings/dashboards allows editing and cloning. IAM User form has Dashboard Type dropdown (required field).

Dashboard_Builder_Page.details: Dashboard Builder loads at /settings/dashboards with 4 system dashboards

System_Dashboard_Templates.details: 4 templates with correct widget counts: Admin (19), Resolver (11), Partner (9), Requester (10)

Widget_Library.details: Widget library with 7 categories: KPI Cards, SLA Status, Pool Status, My Workload, Charts, Ticket Lists, Other

Dashboard_Preview.details: GET /api/dashboards/{id}/preview?role=X filters widgets by role permissions

IAM_Dashboard_Type_Dropdown.details: Dashboard Type dropdown in IAM User form shows all dashboard templates

Widget_Auto_Hide.details: Widgets have required_permissions field for auto-hide based on user permissions

mocked_apis.note: GoHighLevel payment webhook is MOCKED as per previous context
